steps.title: Use relative years to keep the model simple
steps.content: Let $t$ be years after $1995$. Then $1995$ becomes $t=0$, $2015$ becomes $t=20$, and $2019$ becomes $t=24$. The two known data points are $(0,59)$ and $(20,217)$, where population is measured in thousands.

steps.title: Predict the 2019 population
steps.content: The table regression gives $m=7.9$ and $b=59$, so the model is $y=7.9t+59$. For $2019$, use $t=24$: $$y=7.9(24)+59=248.6.$$ To the nearest whole number, this is $249$ thousand.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The question asks for $x$ thousand in $2019$, rounded to the nearest whole number. Since $248.6$ rounds to $249$, the answer is 249.
